predictions.approach: Each leaf is a self-contained, verifiable section of the final brief — gather -> normalise -> synthesise. Knowledge-work, no code.

prd.title: PRD: Competitor-pricing analysis report
prd.body_markdown:
## Objective
Deliver a decision-ready brief comparing our pricing against the top 5 competitors, with a recommended positioning move.

## Why
Sales reports we are losing mid-market deals on perceived price. Leadership needs a grounded view before the Q3 pricing review.

## Constraints
- Cite a primary source (public pricing page / filing) for every number; no unsourced figures.
- Normalise to a common unit (price per seat / month, USD) so tiers compare like-for-like.
- One page of recommendation max — leadership reads the exec summary first.

## Out of scope
- Building a live pricing-monitor tool.
- Win/loss interviews (separate workstream).

## Success definition
A reviewer can trace every comparison cell to a cited source, and the recommendation names one concrete positioning move with its expected trade-off.

## Chosen approach
Three vertical slices: (1) sourced pricing dataset, (2) normalised comparison matrix, (3) synthesis + recommendation. Each is independently checkable.

## Open questions
- Which 5 competitors are in-scope — confirm the set with sales.
- Reference currency / FX date.

## Traceability
Ground consulted: corpus is web pricing pages + a sales brief, not code. codegraph does not apply; graphify (mixed-corpora) NOT installed -> structural grounding UNAVAILABLE. Remediation proposed: install graphify for the source corpus, or accept manual sourcing with per-cell citations (chosen here). Effort below tagged UNGROUNDED (no graph).

leaves.title: Sourced pricing dataset for the 5 competitors

success_criteria.criterion: Every competitor's published tiers captured with a primary-source citation
success_criteria.gradeable_signal: dataset has >=5 competitors, each row carries a source URL + access date

stop_conditions: If a competitor hides pricing behind 'contact sales', STOP and flag it as a data gap rather than estimating.

predictions.approach: Pull each competitor's pricing page, record tiers verbatim + citation. UNGROUNDED effort — no graph consulted.
predictions.pre_mortem: Most likely failure: a competitor's pricing is region-gated and not comparable.

leaves.title: Normalised like-for-like comparison matrix

success_criteria.criterion: All prices normalised to USD per seat per month at a stated FX date
success_criteria.gradeable_signal: matrix has one common unit; every cell traces to a K1 dataset row

stop_conditions: If two competitors' tiers are structurally incomparable (usage vs seat), STOP and surface the modelling choice to the operator.

predictions.approach: Map each tier to USD/seat/month at a fixed FX date; annotate assumptions. UNGROUNDED effort — no graph consulted.
predictions.pre_mortem: Most likely failure: annual-vs-monthly discounting distorts the comparison.

leaves.title: Synthesis and one-page recommendation

success_criteria.criterion: Recommendation names one concrete positioning move with its expected trade-off
success_criteria.gradeable_signal: exec summary states a single move + the trade-off, each claim citing K2 cells

stop_conditions: If the matrix shows no defensible positioning gap, STOP and report 'no change recommended' rather than inventing one.

predictions.approach: Read the matrix, identify the gap, write the exec summary + one-page rec. Human leaf — judgment-heavy, not auto-dispatched.
predictions.pre_mortem: Most likely failure: recommendation is generic ('lower price') without a defensible trade-off.
